This seems to mainly depend on the weather: if it rains  bacteria (E-Coli) get more. Official plan is postponing some events in that case  and even only one such case would be enough for yes. Very low bar  and swimming in there has been forbidden for 100 years  so base rate is horrific.   But... generally water quality in the Seine seems to have become much better (link 1 shows Mayor Hidalgo swimming in there)  and they have two more weeks to clean it up. Plus  they have had probably worse conditions before (Rio  Beijing)  and the IOC did not care.   Unfortunately it´s too early for a reliable weatherforecast. For now it´s only guessing. ------------------------   [redacted to maintain anonymity]

[redacted to maintain anonymity] The nationalist PiS (law and justice) Party is out  except for the President. As was stated  the election in the winter was part of the reason for increased riots. The farmers were protesting violently at the Ukrainian border  blocking the highways and emptying grain from trucks coming from Ukraine. They did the same with trains. The government let this happen during the elections and was slow to put a stop to it. It haas since subsided and the leaders of the farmers political party gave the message that protest is fine  but not at the border blocking traffic and not at the expense of decreasing Ukraines fight against a common enemy. The greatest number of immigrants by far is from Ukraine. Ukraine is predominantly Orthodox Christian  and Poland is predominantly Catholic  so within the same general family. Their languages are different but related. Polands desire to keep the Russian invasion far from their borders gives them a high degree of tolerance for the Ukrainian refugees. If the economy collapses  that can change  but for now their economy is strong and opportunities are abundant. I expect riots to decrease with the seasonal exceptions that have been noted  and near the team of the potential May 2025 election.

The price of a company’s shares is by definition a crowd forecast of its future value. All available company information  including analysis and expectations of the investors  is quickly and efficiently aggregated and incorporated into the stock price. The past performance of a company’s stock is not a reliable predictor of future performance.   Consequently  it is practically impossible to accurately predict the movement of markets in the short term.   For what concerns the long-term  it is possible to make some basic assumptions about the generally growing trajectory of the US economy  and about the fact that companies aim at being part of such growth. Consequently  one would expect stock prices to be at least moderately higher in the long run.   I took the prices for all of the 2014 SP500 components and checked where they stand today to get a basic sense of what is the base rate chance that companies keep growing over the next 5-10 Years. Then  Im skewing to account for the recent performance of individual companies and for general considerations about the sectors they operate in.   AT&Ts stock price has been going up and down in the past few years but did not show organic growth. [redacted to maintain anonymity] there might be technical reasons why this is the case. The company makes both acquisitions and sells some companies. Im not moving from 50% all the way.

I dont understand what this is but Wikipedia page for it says it no longer exists:
The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) is a loan program for banks operated by the United States Federal Reserve since 2023 the Federal Reserve established BTFP to offer loans of up to one year to eligible depository institutions pledging qualifying assets as collateral as a response to help stabilize the banking industry after the 2023 United States banking crisis. The program was introduced on March 12 2023 and was set to expire in March 2024. It ceased extending new loans on March 11 2024.
The graph in the resolution URL is consistent with this statement - the sharp drop is probably loans that still exist but no loans are given. As such its safe to forecast decrease across all dates.

The consensus on this question is all over the place.   The pre-super-swimsuit era record was 21.64s at the beginning of the millennia. In the past 23 years  the time has fallen by about 0.6s. There is still a tenth of a second left to be scraped off Cielos record.   At the average pace of 0.03s per year  the record would most likely be beaten at least 3 years from now. It can certainly happen earlier  at the next Olympics  but I would suspect it to be less likely than a coin flip.   The record is not currently on the list of those likely to fall in Paris.   One also needs to consider the possibility that Cielos record might be revoked due to being obtained during the Speedo LZR era.

Almost no chance:   1. There are currently no vaccines against fungal infections. NONE. There are good biological reasons for this.   2. Luckily  there are a number of fungal-specific antibiotics that tend to be very effective (although it can get very complicated at times).   3. Tinea cruris is not a priority. Vaccines against some various fungal species that do cause deadly diseases are (various species of Candida  Cryptococcus  and Aspergillus). Vaccines against these would be very important but at this point we are not even past a pre-cilinical research exploration.   Bottom line: Only a remote chance ten years from now.

Im not a medical or science professional  but I take it that a Strep A vaccine would suffice as a Cellulitis Vaccine.   Currently  Investment in Strep A vaccine R&D is disproportionately low relative to the large burden of Strep A diseases globally.  Only three vaccine candidates currently in development have completed or recently begun a Phase 1 clinical trial.  There are currently eight candidates on a product development track  including four M protein-based candidates and four candidates designed around non-M protein antigens.   Despite a range of promising candidates  the pipeline of S. pyogenes vaccines remains relatively empty  especially when compared with pipelines for other infectious diseases that cause high global disease burden.   It seems there is a lot that can be used from the worlds experience developing the Covid vaccines.  But there is not a lot of focus on this due to competing diseases with relatively lower costs to develop.  Although progress will be made  Im not sure we will see it before 2030.

1. A good overview: https://www.cdc.gov/hepatitis-d/about/index.html The gist is that the vaccine for Hep B already protects against Hep D (one can only get Hep D if one already has Hep B) it infects a small amount of people engaging in risky behavior and although liver failure can result its typically not lethal.
2. I can find no clinical trials for a Hep D vaccine in process. Theres a greater chance that the Wikipedia editor presses the yes button by mistake than a vaccine is developed for this disease in the foreseeable future.
3. In the more distant future am leaving a small hedge on the possibility of new tech AI or otherwise speeding things up. But new tech isnt going to do away with the need for detailed human trials for new drugs and bureaucratic inertia. Still if the access to drug development tech becomes sufficiently democratized around the world (which would increase bioterror risk as well but thats another discussion)...its possible that some country with less stringent approval standards than the developed world has for new drugs could come up with something? Highly doubtful but will leave 1% on 2034.

As far as I can tell  there is no explicit clarification on the manifold markets site whether or not Plug-in Hybrids count as electric vehicles for the purpose of this question. Im assuming they do not count and only purely electric cars with no combustion engines do.   Recent numbers from China have been very encouraging and given electromobility bulls new hope. China will almost certainly reach that threshhold way before 2030  but most other important markets  notably China and Europe (with big differences within countries) lag behind. In both  I see charging infrastructure as the major chokepoint - these countries have been slow to add large amounts of charging stations and could hardly even deal with that many electric vehicles being added to the fleet quickly. We also see protectionist measures aganst cheap Chinese EV imports  futher hampering sales growth in Europe.   Could it still happen? Yes. There have been substantial price drop in batteries  making electric cars more competitive. Ultimately  the trendline points to the 50% threshhold being reached  but my estimate is that it will be reached only by 2032 or 2033.

Using historical averages is a decent method for forecasting the weather more than 10 days in advance.   I downloaded the csv file from the linked source and created a Montecarlo simulation with the average temperatures of the listed days. 12 years of data is not a lot  so for each day  I averaged the temperature of the previous and following day. This might not be a perfect approach as the temperature of consecutive days might be correlated  but especially during the summer  it would seem most likely that one day was cooler simply because of a shower. I considered a wider spread than what the data suggested.   The results are consistent with intuition. For example  there is roughly a 50% chance that 07/21 would be hotter in 2025 than in 2024 while it is almost impossible that the 17th of January would be hotter than one day in July.   Weather forecasts for next Sunday suggest it will be hotter than average  implying that July dates in upcoming years are just slightly less likely than a coin flip to beat it.   While intuitively one could expect that temperatures are more likely to be higher in 2029 and 2034 as a result of global warming  the effect is almost negligible for this forecast. One could even argue that average temperatures might end up being lower due to increased showers and thunderstorms.

Each of first 6 months of the year was above 2023 temperature by average of 0.47 F. There is a chance that due to El Ninio La ninia anomalies will trigger some cooling down but it would probably require to start in July  otherwise cooling in each subsequent month would need to be very significant vs 2023. And June 2024 was still 0.27F above June 2023. From the other hand July-Dec 2023 were extremly hot so going back even to 2022 temperatures would be enough to be below 2023.

Will there be more than ten times as many fatalities in Niue (a miniature country I had never heard of before) for the 30 days before the dates listed below as compared to one plus the 30-day average of fatalities over the 360 days preceding July 21st  2024 (which was zero)?   In other words: will there be more than 10 times as many fatalities in those 30 days than 1 fatality. Again other words: will there be more than 10 fatalities in the month before the respective dates. ACLED does not count every death as fatality  In the past three years they had zero reported cases. Up to 10 in a month? Unlikely  rounding all bins down to zero.

GB News had operating losses of more than $40 million in the fiscal year that ended in May 2023. It has never been financially sustainable since its launch in 2021. This indicates that there might be a willingness to continue broadcasting despite losses. This could be seen as an “investment” in conservative lobbying in the UK.   The channel would seem unlikely to turn a profit any time soon  but it might be possible that the plug is not pulled until much much later.   The Metaculus community prediction remained around 50/50 for two years  which is inconsistent with the notion that there are now only 5 months left in the question. The bar set by the resolution criteria is relatively low (to forecast for at least 6 hours in 2025).   Farage has recently returned as a host on the channel  suggesting that there might be an intention to continue with the project  at least for a while.

Quick first forecast.   NOAA  predicts an 85% chance of an above-normal season  a 10% chance of a near-normal season and a 5% chance of a below-normal season. NOAA is forecasting a range of 17 to 25 total named storms (winds of 39 mph or higher). [1]   On average  14 named storms occur each season. [2]   Im using NOAAs bins as my base rate and ascribing a 1% chance it will be above their 17-25 named storms threshold.   I would consider going to 0 on a <1% chance rationale.

The program was formally closed as of March 11  2024.  Even prior to that  new loans almost entirely came to a halt after the mini banking crisis of early 2023 had subsided  and the Fed changed the program so it removed the opportunity for rate arbitrage.  Further  projected lower rates may alleviate one of the major issues that caused the liquidity stress  which was a significant amount of mortgage backed securities at banks to have a negative market value to face value  turning a relatively liquid asset into a more illiquid one.  As Fed Funds rates will likely be lowered later this year or in 2024 (given projections for lower inflation)  this should reduce market value losses on such assets and reduce any future need for liquidity from that condition.   It is possible it could be re-opened.  But for that to happen  the Fed would likely need to perceive constrained bank liquidity conditions.  I consider this to be improbable for at least the next couple of years as there are no overt strains on bank liquidity or financial soundness.  A mild recession would not be sufficient.   Further  I would argue that the entire program could be replaced by a new one  specifically designed for any new stress condition.  Under such circumstances  we could only see a run-down of current assets to zero in the future.   So I am going to assume an extremely small probability of increase for all of 2024  a slightly higher one for periods in 2025  and then a significant increase for following years.  But even for the latter periods far in the future  I am going to assume it is still unlikely  given the program itself likely faces extinguishment  and then replacement with a more relevant program designed to face any new financial challenge.

There have been no recorded battles in Laos in the past few years  so the 30-day average is zero. This question resolves as “yes” if there is at least 1 battle in the 30 days preceding the listed dates.   There is no indication that a direct conflict between two armed groups in Laos could happen shortly. The country has been peaceful since 1976  at the end of the civil war.   There have been sparse episodes of violence against civilians in the past few years  as Laos appears to be one of the most repressive countries in the world. Instances of civil unrest and protest have intensified in the past couple of years as a result of dire economic conditions: GDP growth has been slowing significatively  inflation is high  and the currency is on a devaluation free fall.   While things seem to be slightly better in 2024  the consequences of reckless public spending of the corrupted government might create the conditions for an armed revolt.   It is also worth mentioning that there are about half a million ethnic Hmong people who are hiding in the jungle from the persecution of the army which targets villages with shelling and incursions. There are groups of Hmong insurgents rebelling against the Laotian persecution which occasionally clash with the army of Laos. While these events are sparse and small in scale  it is plausible that future confrontations get classified as proper battles.

Rapid chess champion in 2019. Youngest woman GM ever at 15  beating Judit Polgars record. Top rating 2606 in 2007. 37 years old - chess is not a game to stay at the top as people get older. It requires extreme concentration and physical endurance. Comeback after maternity leave. Blitz my counterintuitvely be a better game at her still relative young age  because her matter of positions and the game  while still having quick mental and physical reflexes  will not encounter exhaustion of long classical games. If she is concentrating on rapid and blitz chess she will have less time for classical  which is necessary to maintain rating. Her rating stabilised during Covid time at 2586. Since then her standard  blitz  and rapid rating have experienced a slight decline. She is currently at 2530. A 1% increase would need to be 2556. That is below her scores since 2006 with the brief exception of maternity leave and her current rating which has been for less than a month. A loss to a person a few hundred points lower can upend a score.

3 storms so far. 13.75 is the historical average between halfway through July and the end of the season. The base rate forecast using a Poisson distribution is about 14%. Empirically  this happened only once (2021) in the past 20 seasons.   Meteorological agencies predict a very active hurricane season with a high number of named storms (22 according to UKMO  21 according to NOAA  26 according to TSR  25 according to CSU  23 according to UA). Averaging these forecasts and using the NOAA 70% confidence interval of 17-25 outputs a 33% prediction using Poisson distribution.

Im not a medical or science professional  but I take it that a Strep A vaccine would suffice as a Cellulitis Vaccine.   Currently  Investment in Strep A vaccine R&D is disproportionately low relative to the large burden of Strep A diseases globally.  Only three vaccine candidates currently in development have completed or recently begun a Phase 1 clinical trial.  There are currently eight candidates on a product development track  including four M protein-based candidates and four candidates designed around non-M protein antigens.   Despite a range of promising candidates  the pipeline of S. pyogenes vaccines remains relatively empty  especially when compared with pipelines for other infectious diseases that cause high global disease burden.   It seems there is a lot that can be used from the worlds experience developing the Covid vaccines.  But there is not a lot of focus on this due to competing diseases with relatively lower costs to develop.  Although progress will be made  Im not sure we will see it before 2030.

The price of a company’s shares is by definition a crowd forecast of its future value. All available company information  including analysis and expectations of the investors  is quickly and efficiently aggregated and incorporated into the stock price. The past performance of a company’s stock is not a reliable predictor of future performance.   Consequently  it is practically impossible to accurately predict the movement of markets in the short term.   For what concerns the long-term  it is possible to make some basic assumptions about the generally growing trajectory of the US economy  and about the fact that companies aim at being part of such growth. Consequently  one would expect stock prices to be at least moderately higher in the long run.   I took the prices for all of the 2014 SP500 components and checked where they stand today to get a basic sense of what is the base rate chance that companies keep growing over the next 5-10 Years. Then  Im skewing to account for the recent performance of individual companies and for general considerations about the sectors they operate in.   Tractor Supply Co. operates in the retail sector  selling products specific to agriculture  home improvement  and gardening. The stock performed decently in the past. The agriculture sector is not a growing industry. Gardening and home improvement products are discretionaries that might suffer in a bad turn of the economy. Retail industries have also suffered particularly in recent years with the advent of large online e-commerce  and the recent increase in imports of these types of products from China.
Still below where it was in 2012  not a good sign. But 10-year trend more likely to be up  like the overall market

Temperatures have increase overall about 1.6 degrees during this timeframe.   For the first two buckets  i am going to assume that temperatures will more likely be higher later in the summer.  So I have put in an above 50% probability of this happening.   For the next two  I am assuming that temperatures will be lower in the fall (October)  and much lower in January.   For the last three  I assume that
January and October are virtually never warmer than July. October could have a low chance if July 21 is cool and Oct 19 is warm. July and August are likely  50/50 shot each day.

Northern hemisphere  so July and August are the warmest months there  about the same long-term. October (autumn) mostly colder (quite some variation in there) and January (winter) much colder. The ELO ratings of chess players tend to grow over time and peak in their late 20s  then slowly decline. Sarasadat Khademalsharieh is 27 years old  and her ELO rating remained quite stable in the past 5 years.   Intuition suggests that her ELO rating is likely to keep stagnating in the short term and then it will descend more rapidly after a few years.   The resulting forecast is not easy for one specific reason: a prediction has to be made about the chances that the rating goes up by at least 1% which is 25 points. In the past 5 years  there was never a moment in which her ELO rating grew by that many points. The likelihood of such growth over a short amount of time is low  but it might be possible that such progress materializes in the next few years.
The rate seems to have peaked and with expected rate cut. Very unlikely in the short term but inflation may happen in the long term  increasing gradually.

The consensus on this question is all over the place.   The pre-super-swimsuit era record was 21.64s at the beginning of the millennia. In the past 23 years  the time has fallen by about 0.6s. There is still a tenth of a second left to be scraped off Cielos record.   At the average pace of 0.03s per year  the record would most likely be beaten at least 3 years from now. It can certainly happen earlier  at the next Olympics  but I would suspect it to be less likely than a coin flip.   The record is not currently on the list of those likely to fall in Paris.   One also needs to consider the possibility that Cielos record might be revoked due to being obtained during the Speedo LZR era.

In the near term  which I define as up until 1/17/25  I am assuming market forces will be moving the price.  For the first bucket I am assuming pure randomness as I have no idea what the market will do as of that date.  For the second  third  and fourth dates I am assuming a slightly positive market bias  as I am assuming it is a small cap stock (revenues about $1.7 billion)  and with lower rates coming this should be beneficial.  In the longer term starting from 7/21/25  I am assuming general growth in price expected from stocks over time will be a tail-wind carrying the probability gradually higher.  One assumption driving my longer term bias is this is a viable going concern for many years to come.  The product should see increased demand and the company is currently financially sound.

The price of a company’s shares is by definition a crowd forecast of its future value. All available company information  including analysis and expectations of the investors  is quickly and efficiently aggregated and incorporated into the stock price. The past performance of a company’s stock is not a reliable predictor of future performance.   Consequently  it is practically impossible to accurately predict the movement of markets in the short term.   For what concerns the long-term  it is possible to make some basic assumptions about the generally growing trajectory of the US economy  and about the fact that companies aim at being part of such growth. Consequently  one would expect stock prices to be at least moderately higher in the long run.   I took the prices for all of the 2014 SP500 components and checked where they stand today to get a basic sense of what is the base rate chance that companies keep growing over the next 5-10 Years. Then  Im skewing to account for the recent performance of individual companies and for general considerations about the sectors they operate in.   Cintas is a service company that provides uniforms and equipment to a wide range of clients  including the government. The company has performed strongly in the past and keeps growing through acquisitions. There are no predictable threats that could emerge in their market in the upcoming years  so Im skewing the distribution toward better performance.
